extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r1, whose IF clause is the conjunction of 12 conditions, one of which is immunosuppressed. rec_drug_b is set true by r3 whenever immunosuppressed is true. Each output has exactly one rule that can set it true, so priorities/ties never block them and defaults only apply when an output is left unset. Therefore whenever r1's full conjunction holds, immunosuppressed is true, so r3 also fires: rec_drug_a = true and rec_drug_b = true simultaneously, violating mutual exclusion. The counterexample sets all 12 r1 conditions true (heart_failure irrelevant, set false). r1 sets rec_drug_a=true; r3 sets rec_drug_b=true; property (not (rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b)) is violated.
